personas.name: Singapore HNW longevity-curious
personas.icp: 42–60 yo SG resident with household income S$500k+; lives Sentosa Cove / D9 / D10 / Bukit Timah; finance / law / family-business / C-suite. Already engaged with longevity content (~50% per Manulife 2024); aware of Prenuvo and Neko but has not booked. Holds SG passport, comfortable with cross-border, likely already a regular JB visitor for golf / dining.
pains: Mt Elizabeth Excelsior at S$12,388 reads as 'paying twice for a hospital that won’t feel concierge'
Hospital screening feels transactional and full-day; cannot fit into a discrete weekend slot
Distrusts SG hospital upsell pattern (recurring Reddit r/singapore complaint)
Refuses to fly to US/UK/Stockholm just for an MRI — friction kills the whole intent
Has heard of Prenuvo/Ezra/Neko but cannot explain the difference; wants a credible local synthesis
personas.current_workaround: Annual hospital screening at Raffles or Mt Elizabeth (~S$11-13k including endoscopy + cardiac), or AsiaMedic Navigator 7 at S$4,600 if cost-conscious. Some have done one Prenuvo trip while in LA / NYC. Most are still in 'I’ll get a scan eventually' inertia.

nba.summary: Anchored to AsiaMedic Navigator 7 (S$4,600) — the closest like-for-like SG standalone advanced-imaging alternative this persona would pick if they didn’t cross to JB. Per-scan-event displaced spend; presented monthly-equivalent for cross-persona comparability under the FIELD-DICTIONARY arithmetic.

personas.name: Indonesian HNW with SG family office
personas.icp: 35–65 yo, family business / banking / commodities / property; SG family office structure with kids in SG schools; already routes through SG private bankers (Mandiri Private, BCA Prioritas, DBS Treasures, Standard Chartered Priority Private). Travels SG ↔ Jakarta multiple times per year; pairs medical with retail / private banking trips.
pains: Indonesia private hospital quality is uneven; Jakarta best-in-class still feels behind SG / MY top tier
Singapore hospital pricing reads as 2-3× fair value; the markup feels punitive
Wants discretion — lounge experience matters as much as clinical depth
Family travels together; medical visit must accommodate spouse + 1-2 adult children (4-day average trip)
Bahasa Indonesia patient comms (consent forms, results explanation, family briefing) is a non-trivial preference
personas.current_workaround: Mt Elizabeth Novena annual screening (~S$11-13k for the principal), Pantai KL or Sunway Velocity for follow-up imaging, Bali wellness retreats for the spouse. Family treats medical visits as part of a longer SG/JB long-weekend.

nba.summary: Anchored to Mt Elizabeth Novena hospital concierge screen ~S$11,000 (Indonesian-track pricing, includes lounge + bilingual handler). ID HNW already pays the SG hospital ceiling for the concierge; AEVUM Legacy Ultimate at S$5,300 substitutes ~52% of that displaced spend at a more discreet multi-day wrapper.

personas.name: Malaysian Affluent professional
personas.icp: 35–55 yo, professional / SME owner / corporate executive in JB / KL / Penang; RM 50k+ household monthly income; uses Gleneagles / Sunway / Pantai for routine premium care; reads The Edge, Tatler MY, Business Today; books via WhatsApp.
pains: Existing JB hospital screening (KPJ, Mahkota, Gleneagles JB) feels clinical-utility, not concierge
Wants premium screening without flying to KL or SG — time-poor
Curious about Prenuvo / Neko but no MY presence and the brands feel 'foreign'
Loyal to Gleneagles / Sunway brand equity — needs to earn trust before switching
Wants something to gift parents (they have hospital fatigue)
personas.current_workaround: Gleneagles Johor or KPJ Bandar Dato’ Onn for routine MRI (RM 1,200-2,000); Sunway Medical Velocity KL for premium screening (~S$2,400-equivalent); occasional ad-hoc imaging at Pantai KL.

nba.summary: Anchored to Sunway Medical Centre Velocity (KL) premium screening ~S$2,400 — the MY-side reference for what 'premium concierge MRI' currently looks like for this persona. AEVUM Comprehensive (S$2,640) lands within ~10% of Sunway Velocity at a JB-proximate, MRI-led wrapper.

personas.name: C-Suite / Executive Program buyer (B2B)
personas.icp: Corporate HR / Group HR / family-office / single-family-office / private-equity portfolio operator buying for an executive cohort or principal family. Procurement-mediated; typical RFP cycle 6-12 months; buying for 10-50 named scans per year as part of executive comp + risk-management.
pains: SG hospital corporate health programs (Raffles HealthShield, Parkway corporate) are commodity — nothing to put on the offer letter that signals care
Singapore screening providers don’t differentiate for the C-suite tier
Wants a defensible, recurring program with named radiologist relationship, not a one-off
Risk-management framing matters more than wellness framing — 'protecting the principal'
Procurement requires a paper trail (vendor compliance, PDPA / PDP, audit-ready invoicing)
personas.current_workaround: Raffles or Parkway corporate health-screening retainer (~S$80-300/employee/yr for basic screening), with ad-hoc top-ups for the C-suite at Mt Elizabeth or AsiaMedic. PE family-offices use Mt Elizabeth Novena for principals (~S$15-30k/yr per principal).

nba.summary: Anchored to Raffles Platinum Executive Screening S$11,408 retail per principal. Corporate-rate negotiations land at ~50-65% of retail = S$5,500-7,400/principal/yr. Founders Programme per-principal displaced spend modelled at the retail anchor since most family-office buyers benchmark against retail not negotiated-rate.

pricing_models.name: Tiered package pricing (Core / Comprehensive / Ultimate / Platinum)
pricing_models.rationale: AEVUM’s core model. Aligns CapEx (1.5T vs 3T magnet) with tier; anchors against SG hospital concierge ceiling; MAB-compliant (bundled pricing + package discounts permitted); easy upsell from Core to Comprehensive over 12 months. Mirrors Prenuvo’s tier ladder in structure but localised on SGD/MYR pricing and concierge inclusions.

pricing_models.name: Concierge / experience premium wrapper
pricing_models.rationale: Bundle scan + RTS Link transfer + lounge + post-scan radiologist time as one price. Justifies 35-40% premium versus stripped-down hospital MRI. Pairs with Model 01 — every tier 'concierge-included' rather than as an upsell. Echelon Health Mayfair-style.

pricing_models.name: Annual membership / subscription
pricing_models.rationale: RM 8,000-15,000/yr for one annual scan + quarterly biomarker pulls + 24/7 health concierge. Y2 unlock once retention discipline proven. SG/MY HNW culturally prefer one-off purchases over subs (BoardRoom 2025 survey: 63% prefer pay-per-procedure); MAB scrutiny on 'membership' framing for medical service.

pricing_models.name: Family / couple / two-generation bundle
pricing_models.rationale: 'Two for the price of 1.5' couple bundle, parent + adult-child legacy bundle. Strong gifting framing for HNW Asian families; doubles concierge throughput per visit. Launch as MAB-compliant seasonal promo (Mother’s Day, Father’s Day, CNY).

pricing_models.name: Founders Programme — Corporate / family-office retainer
pricing_models.rationale: B2B contract: family office, single-family office, PE portfolio, or corporate executive program. RM 200,000-500,000/yr for n=20-40 guaranteed scans. Predictable utilisation; aligns with Elitez Group’s existing B2B sales muscle; MAB-permitted (corporate health programs).

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Manulife SG Affluent Insights 2024 + Reddit r/singapore HNW screening threads: explicit price-walk above S$5,000 for an annual screen unless the concierge wrapper is exceptional; most cite S$2,500-3,000 as 'sensible annual' and the S$11k+ hospital tier as 'paying for the brand, not the value'. AEVUM Comprehensive at S$2,640 sits at the dead centre of this band; Legacy Ultimate at S$5,300 needs concierge + hospitality to justify.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: Indonesian HNW with SG family office
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: PwC SEA Wellness Tourism 2025 + MHTC Indonesian Patient Profile 2024: ID HNW pay 50-100% premium for 'discreet, family-friendly, non-clinical' provider. Once trust is established, price sensitivity collapses. Sunway Medical Velocity’s ID patient ARPU is ~US$3,000-5,000 versus ~US$1,500 for the local-MY ARPU. Walk-away threshold is closer to ~S$15k for the principal.

DOSM 2022 + Tatler MY 2025 affluent-spending column: MY affluent benchmark new vendor pricing against Gleneagles / Sunway as references (RM 1,500-2,500 for premium MRI). Above RM 8,000 (~S$2,400) requires explicit premium justification (concierge + named radiologist + same-day results). AEVUM Core (RM 1,999) hits the natural starting price; Comprehensive (RM 8,699) needs concierge story to land.

Aon SG Health Survey 2025 + Mercer SG Reward 2025: corporate principals on executive health programs are price-INSENSITIVE — the procurement frame is 'what does HR / GP / GP think the firm should commit to', not 'what is the cheapest option'. PE-portfolio principals routinely run S$15-30k/yr per principal for medical retainers (KPMG Family Office SG 2024). Walk-away is closer to S$10k/principal/yr; everything below that is procurement-friendly.

recommended_tiers.name: Core Scan
what_in: Whole-body MRI (single session, ~45-60 min on Siemens 1.5T)
Named consultant radiologist read with NSR-listed credential
Same-day or next-day digital report
Concierge welcome + private changing suite
MAB-compliant scan-summary delivered in EN/BM at the patient’s preference
what_excluded: RTS Link transfer + return ticket (upgrade to Comprehensive)
Multi-modality bloods + biomarkers (upgrade to Legacy Ultimate)
Hospitality / hotel partnership package (upgrade to Legacy Platinum)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$575 is below the entry-level SG hospital MRI add-on (~S$1,090 at Healthscreening.sg) for a fully-concierge scan with named radiologist read — a price the JB-resident professional or first-time SG visitor commits to without spousal sign-off.

recommended_tiers.name: Comprehensive
what_in: Everything in Core Scan, plus:
Whole-body MRI on Siemens 3T (higher-resolution magnet)
RTS Link return ticket + AEVUM-side transfer included (post-RTS opening 2026)
30-minute named-consultant-radiologist video walkthrough (post-scan, scheduled within 5 days)
Cardiac structural sequence + neurovascular sequence add-on
Annual baseline-tracking report (year-on-year comparison if returning patient)
Bahasa Indonesia / Bahasa Melayu consultation upgrade on request
what_excluded: Multi-day stay + hospitality bundle (upgrade to Legacy Ultimate / Platinum)
Family-office concierge (named relationship manager, family-language briefing)
Big-4-style independent valuation deliverable (not applicable to medical)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$2,640 is roughly one-fifth of Mt Elizabeth Screen Excelsior (S$12,388) and Raffles Platinum (S$11,408) for an MRI-led longevity scan that they don’t deliver — the right SG HNW notch for the 'I should make this annual' commitment.

recommended_tiers.name: Legacy Ultimate
what_in: Everything in Comprehensive, plus:
Optional 1-night stay at AEVUM hotel partner (DoubleTree JB / Renaissance JB / Mid Valley Southkey)
Private-driver transfer (SG-side pick-up + RTS or causeway routing)
Family lounge for accompanying spouse + 1-2 adult children
Multi-modality biomarker panel (~50 markers including ApoB, CRP, HbA1c, hormone panel)
Named-consultant relationship at the multi-year baseline level
Annual longevity coach 30-min consult (lifestyle + nutrition + sleep)
Family-language briefing in Bahasa Indonesia / Bahasa Melayu / Mandarin where requested
what_excluded: Big-4 audit deliverable (not applicable to medical)
Medical concierge for chronic-disease management (separate tier)
Immediate-family extension to additional members beyond 3 (priced separately)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$5,300 is 35% below the Echelon Health Mayfair concierge tier (£14,000) for an Asia-based equivalent that adds RTS Link + family-friendly hospitality. Below Raffles Platinum by 50% — the right notch for the Indonesian HNW family-office buyer who wants the experience to feel 'discreet hotel' not 'busy hospital'.

recommended_tiers.name: Legacy Platinum
what_in: Everything in Legacy Ultimate, plus:
Multi-day (2-night) hospitality bundle with curated JB hotel partner
Pre-scan longevity-baseline consult (90 min, in person)
Whole-genome biomarker pull (~120 markers including expanded cancer-risk panel)
12-month follow-up year of named consultant access (quarterly check-in)
Concierge transport (SG private-driver pick-up + return) included
Family member upgrade pricing (accompanying scan for spouse / parent at 30% discount)
Founders-tier waiting lounge upgrade with private dining
what_excluded: Beyond-12-month annual membership (a separate Y2 SKU)
M&A or exit-event due-diligence support (not applicable)
Custom corporate executive program (Founders Programme channel)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$7,420 is 35% below Mt Elizabeth Excelsior (S$12,388) for an experience that meaningfully outclasses the hospital concierge — multi-day, named-consultant, family-friendly. Equivalent to a 5-night Chenot stay (CHF 5,300-6,900). The aspirational ceiling that lets the SG HNW longevity-curious upgrade once they trust the brand.
